texts.label: text
texts.orig: Below is a list of patches that you must verify are properly installed on your system before OR*3.0*312 can be installed:
texts.text: Below is a list of patches that you must verify are properly installed on your system before OR*3.0*312 can be installed:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: OR*3.0*312 will be released using the National Patch Module. The Kernel Installation and Distribution (KIDS) build will be a part of the PackMan message that will be distributed.
texts.text: OR*3.0*312 will be released using the National Patch Module. The Kernel Installation and Distribution (KIDS) build will be a part of the PackMan message that will be distributed.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Installation instructions are included in the patch description.
texts.text: Installation instructions are included in the patch description.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If your site has made any local changes to entries in the OE/RR REPORTS file (#101.24) with an internal entry number greater than 999, you will need to save those changes off and redo them after installing this patch. Or, you will need to move the changed entries to internal entry numbers less than 999.
texts.text: If your site has made any local changes to entries in the OE/RR REPORTS file (#101.24) with an internal entry number greater than 999, you will need to save those changes off and redo them after installing this patch. Or, you will need to move the changed entries to internal entry numbers less than 999.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PSPO 970: Inpatient Meds Data Not Complete in Medications View
texts.text: PSPO 970: Inpatient Meds Data Not Complete in Medications View

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This patient safety issue is resolved by patches from the VistAWeb and Computerized Patient Record System (CPRS) applications. The VistAWeb application first released a patch that contained a warning and then CPRS is releasing patch OR*3.0*312 that distributes a new report. The same data from the report will be available to VistAWeb and through Remote Data Views in CPRS.
texts.text: This patient safety issue is resolved by patches from the VistAWeb and Computerized Patient Record System (CPRS) applications. The VistAWeb application first released a patch that contained a warning and then CPRS is releasing patch OR*3.0*312 that distributes a new report. The same data from the report will be available to VistAWeb and through Remote Data Views in CPRS.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: First, VistAWeb released patch WEBV*1*13 on 11/12/08 and promoted it to production server on 11/12/08 9pm.
texts.text: First, VistAWeb released patch WEBV*1*13 on 11/12/08 and promoted it to production server on 11/12/08 9pm.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: From the patch description: VistAWeb V8.0 includes the following enhancements and fixes: 1. Resolved PSP0-970 (Remedy #259376) - Inpatient Medications from a previous VA inpatient stay may not display in Remote Data View (RDV) and VistAWeb "Pharmacy - Medications" view of medications, depending on the current VA patient status.
texts.text: From the patch description: VistAWeb V8.0 includes the following enhancements and fixes: 1. Resolved PSP0-970 (Remedy #259376) - Inpatient Medications from a previous VA inpatient stay may not display in Remote Data View (RDV) and VistAWeb "Pharmacy - Medications" view of medications, depending on the current VA patient status.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: With this patch, the VistAWeb “Pharmacy -> Medications” report displays the following warning:
texts.text: With this patch, the VistAWeb “Pharmacy -> Medications” report displays the following warning:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: NOTE: INPATIENT medications with a status other than ACTIVE may not display in this view. Please see the Unit Dose and ALL IV reports for a comprehensive list of the patient's current and recent inpatient medications.
texts.text: NOTE: INPATIENT medications with a status other than ACTIVE may not display in this view. Please see the Unit Dose and ALL IV reports for a comprehensive list of the patient's current and recent inpatient medications.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In addition to the VistAWeb changes, OR*3.0*312 makes changes to address this problem. Specifically, this patch adds a new All Medications report to the Pharmacy section of Clinical Reports on the Reports Tab in CPRS.
texts.text: In addition to the VistAWeb changes, OR*3.0*312 makes changes to address this problem. Specifically, this patch adds a new All Medications report to the Pharmacy section of Clinical Reports on the Reports Tab in CPRS.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The All Medications report includes All Inpatient and Outpatient Pharmacy data for a patient. The user can limit the data in the report by using date range parameters. To determine the data that is included, based on date range selection, the logic is to first look for Last Fill Date, then Issue Date, then Order date (depends on if the date exists for the drug/RX being screened).
texts.text: The All Medications report includes All Inpatient and Outpatient Pharmacy data for a patient. The user can limit the data in the report by using date range parameters. To determine the data that is included, based on date range selection, the logic is to first look for Last Fill Date, then Issue Date, then Order date (depends on if the date exists for the drug/RX being screened).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The report is initially sorted by STATUS in the following order:
texts.text: The report is initially sorted by STATUS in the following order:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: And then alphabetically within each of those groups.
texts.text: And then alphabetically within each of those groups.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Users can also sort the by selecting a column header, thus sorting by that column.
texts.text: Users can also sort the by selecting a column header, thus sorting by that column.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The All Medications report will be placed as the first item on the Pharmacy menu on the CPRS Reports tab.
texts.text: The All Medications report will be placed as the first item on the Pharmacy menu on the CPRS Reports tab.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The All Medications report supports Remote Data Views (RDV) from remote Facilities.
texts.text: The All Medications report supports Remote Data Views (RDV) from remote Facilities.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Remote data will not be available from all sites until every VA site has installed this patch. Only sites that have installed this patch will return remote data for this report when being queried. If a remote site is queried that has not installed the patch, no data is returned and no message is given to the user indicating that the remote site may or may not have data. This will not be a problem once all sites have installed the patch, usually within 30 days of the patch being released.
texts.text: Remote data will not be available from all sites until every VA site has installed this patch. Only sites that have installed this patch will return remote data for this report when being queried. If a remote site is queried that has not installed the patch, no data is returned and no message is given to the user indicating that the remote site may or may not have data. This will not be a problem once all sites have installed the patch, usually within 30 days of the patch being released.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The API's (Application Program Interface) created by this effort will also be used by the VistaWeb application to create the same report in VistaWeb.  VistaWeb is a web based inter-facility patient reporting application.
texts.text: The API's (Application Program Interface) created by this effort will also be used by the VistaWeb application to create the same report in VistaWeb.  VistaWeb is a web based inter-facility patient reporting application.

texts.text: Example of the All Medications report:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This patch changes the Date Range selection logic for the Pharmacy All Outpatient report.  Prior to this change, the report did not honor any date selection but would display all data when the report was selected.  With this change, the user can specify a date range for pulling outpatient pharmacy data. To determine the medications that are included, based on date range selection, CPRS looks at
texts.text: This patch changes the Date Range selection logic for the Pharmacy All Outpatient report.  Prior to this change, the report did not honor any date selection but would display all data when the report was selected.  With this change, the user can specify a date range for pulling outpatient pharmacy data. To determine the medications that are included, based on date range selection, CPRS looks at

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Order date (depends on if the date exists for the drug/RX being screened).
texts.text: Order date (depends on if the date exists for the drug/RX being screened).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This change is being made at the request of the Department of Defense (DoD) BHIE AHLTA Project to allow more flexibility and so that Date Range parameters are passed to external interfaces like VistaWeb and DoD queries.
texts.text: This change is being made at the request of the Department of Defense (DoD) BHIE AHLTA Project to allow more flexibility and so that Date Range parameters are passed to external interfaces like VistaWeb and DoD queries.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As part of this change the name of the report is being changed in CPRS to more accurately reflect the data being selected/displayed. The report name will change from "Pharmacy All Outpatient" to simply "Outpatient Medications".  To be consistent, VistaWeb will also be making this change sometime in the future.
texts.text: As part of this change the name of the report is being changed in CPRS to more accurately reflect the data being selected/displayed. The report name will change from "Pharmacy All Outpatient" to simply "Outpatient Medications".  To be consistent, VistaWeb will also be making this change sometime in the future.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This report not only exists on the Dept. of Defense Reports, but also on the Clinical Reports | Pharmacy menu.  The same change to the report name will also be made there.
texts.text: This report not only exists on the Dept. of Defense Reports, but also on the Clinical Reports | Pharmacy menu.  The same change to the report name will also be made there.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: All Reports under Dept. of Defense Now Have Remote Data Only:
texts.text: All Reports under Dept. of Defense Now Have Remote Data Only:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In the past, most of the DoD reports under the Dept. of  Defense category would include local VA data when the report was selected. With the installation of this patch, Local VA data will not be included with any of the DoD reports found under the Dept. of Defense Reports menu. It will still be possible to get local data, as well as DoD data, on the same display for these reports by selecting the same report under the Clinical Reports menu.
texts.text: In the past, most of the DoD reports under the Dept. of  Defense category would include local VA data when the report was selected. With the installation of this patch, Local VA data will not be included with any of the DoD reports found under the Dept. of Defense Reports menu. It will still be possible to get local data, as well as DoD data, on the same display for these reports by selecting the same report under the Clinical Reports menu.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: For example, if the user goes to the Dept. of Defense category and chooses Allergies, the report will include remote DoD and remote VA data, but no local VA data. Under Clinical Reports, the Allergies report will include remote DoD data, remote VA data, and local VA data.
texts.text: For example, if the user goes to the Dept. of Defense category and chooses Allergies, the report will include remote DoD and remote VA data, but no local VA data. Under Clinical Reports, the Allergies report will include remote DoD data, remote VA data, and local VA data.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In addition, some reports are specific to the DoD (e.g., Family History), and the VA does not have a comparable report. Therefore, these reports are “DoD Remote data only”.
texts.text: In addition, some reports are specific to the DoD (e.g., Family History), and the VA does not have a comparable report. Therefore, these reports are “DoD Remote data only”.

texts.text: Example of the Outpatient Medications report:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This patch fixes a potential problem with printed reports that show the Lab Accession number in the header. Accession numbers that extend beyond 10 characters got truncated when printed at the top of the report. For example, an Accession # of "SPLB 03 1648" was printing as "SPLB 03 16".  This patch corrects this problem by extending the Accession # column in the OE/RR Reports File (#101.24) to 16 characters for all reports that contain the Accession # column.
texts.text: This patch fixes a potential problem with printed reports that show the Lab Accession number in the header. Accession numbers that extend beyond 10 characters got truncated when printed at the top of the report. For example, an Accession # of "SPLB 03 1648" was printing as "SPLB 03 16".  This patch corrects this problem by extending the Accession # column in the OE/RR Reports File (#101.24) to 16 characters for all reports that contain the Accession # column.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This patch re-exports entries with internal numbers greater than 999 and entries 3 (ORRP AP ALL) and 24 (ORL ANATOMIC PATHOLOGY) from the OE/RR Reports file (#101.24).  This is a common occurrence when new entries are being sent out in this file.  Entries above 999 are considered nationally controlled entries in this file.  Locally created entries in this file are generally done in entry numbers less than 999.  If you have created or modified entries in this file with an internal number greater than 999 or #3 and #24, those changes will be lost.
texts.text: This patch re-exports entries with internal numbers greater than 999 and entries 3 (ORRP AP ALL) and 24 (ORL ANATOMIC PATHOLOGY) from the OE/RR Reports file (#101.24).  This is a common occurrence when new entries are being sent out in this file.  Entries above 999 are considered nationally controlled entries in this file.  Locally created entries in this file are generally done in entry numbers less than 999.  If you have created or modified entries in this file with an internal number greater than 999 or #3 and #24, those changes will be lost.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To preserve any local changes, either save them off someplace before installing this patch, or move the changed entries to internal numbers less than 999.
texts.text: To preserve any local changes, either save them off someplace before installing this patch, or move the changed entries to internal numbers less than 999.
